description: An action with an unsafe block that allocates a raw buffer and frees it. Validated against the real transpiler across 20 variations (different action/buffer names and sizes), all parse/emit/compile clean.

buffer.desc: the name bound to the allocated buffer

template:
action {action_name} produces nothing
    unsafe
        [RAW_MALLOC: {size} : {buffer}]
        [RAW_FREE: {buffer}]
    end unsafe
end action
requires: RAW_MALLOC's parameters are ordered (size, name-to-bind) -- size first, then the identifier being allocated.
Every RAW_MALLOC in a block should be paired with a RAW_FREE on the same buffer before the block ends.
common_mistakes: Do not swap the parameter order to `[RAW_MALLOC :{buffer}:{size}]` (name first, size second) -- a real test run produced exactly this reversed order.

Do not substitute an ordinary `call` statement (e.g. `call {action_name}`) for the unsafe-token syntax -- RAW_MALLOC/RAW_FREE are not action calls. A real test run produced `call unsafe_demo` (twice) instead of the RAW_MALLOC/RAW_FREE tokens shown here, when no other simple statement was described for the chunk.
example:
action unsafe_demo produces nothing
    unsafe
        [RAW_MALLOC: 100 : Buffer]
        [RAW_FREE: Buffer]
    end unsafe
end action
